machinery_genes.role: first gene of the H2-T6SS core machinery operon (PA1656); membrane/baseplate-associated component
machinery_genes.quote: we analyzed the DNA region upstream of the first gene, hsiA2 (PA1656), of the cluster in PAO1
machinery_genes.note: Sana et al 2012, the definitive PAO1 H2-T6SS characterization. Figure 1 legend states the cluster genes are 'labeled hsiA2 to hsiJ2 and, when applicable, with the given name, i.e., clpV2 or sfa2'. hsiA2 = PA1656 is the first gene of the H2-T6SS machinery operon. Filloux-lab hsiA naming; broadly the TssA/membrane-anchor region of the locus.

machinery_genes.role: contractile tail sheath subunit (inner sheath, VipA/VipB homolog)
machinery_genes.quote: Each of the three P. aeruginosa T6SSs encodes a VipA and a VipB homologue, namely HsiB1 and HsiC1 (H1-T6SS), HsiB2 and HsiC2 (H2-T6SS), and HsiB3 and HsiC3 (H3-T6SS)
machinery_genes.note: Lossi et al 2013 names HsiB2/HsiC2 as the H2-T6SS VipA/VipB (sheath) homologs. The paper structurally characterizes the H1 ortholog (HsiB1C1) as the bacteriophage-tail-sheath-like structure; HsiB2 detected by antibody and induced as an H2 component in Jones et al 2014 (PMID 24317402, 'anti-HsiB2'). In the unified scheme VipA=TssB.

machinery_genes.role: contractile tail sheath subunit (outer sheath, VipA/VipB homolog)
machinery_genes.quote: Each of the three P. aeruginosa T6SSs encodes a VipA and a VipB homologue, namely HsiB1 and HsiC1 (H1-T6SS), HsiB2 and HsiC2 (H2-T6SS), and HsiB3 and HsiC3 (H3-T6SS)
machinery_genes.note: Lossi et al 2013. HsiC2 = H2-T6SS VipB/TssC sheath partner of HsiB2. The H1 ortholog HsiC1 shown to carry the gp18-like sheath domain; same architecture transfers to the H2 system.

Jones et al 2014 (Filloux lab, Imperial). hsiF2 is named as a core H2-T6SS gene and used as a qRT-PCR target probing expression of the core PA14 H2-T6SS cluster (which is conserved between PAO1 and PA14 across the core machinery genes per Fig 1). hsiF = TssF baseplate per the Filloux nomenclature.
Jones et al 2014. hsiG2 named as a core H2-T6SS gene and used as a qRT-PCR expression target for the core H2 cluster. hsiG = TssG baseplate.

machinery_genes.role: AAA+ ATPase that recycles the contracted sheath; essential for H2-T6SS function
machinery_genes.quote: we constructed a nonpolar mutant in the AAA+ ATPase ClpV2 whose homologue was shown to play a crucial role in the functionality of the H1-T6SS system
machinery_genes.note: Sana et al 2012. ClpV2 (encoded by clpV2 within the PAO1 H2-T6SS cluster) is the H2 ClpV/TssH ATPase; its deletion (PAO1 delta-clpV2) abolishes H2-T6SS-dependent internalization. Marks the distal end of the core cluster (deletions span 'mid-hsiA2 to mid-clpV2', Jones 2014).

machinery_genes.role: secreted hexameric tube protein (inner tube of the firing apparatus)
machinery_genes.quote: A specific set of four genes encodes an Hcp protein (Hcp2), a VgrG protein (VgrG14), an Rhs element (PA14_43100 or RhsP2)
machinery_genes.note: Jones et al 2014. Hcp2 is the H2-T6SS Hcp/tube; its T6SS-dependent secretion (lost in delta-H2) demonstrates an assembled H2 apparatus. In PAO1 the associated Hcp is named Hcp2a (PA1512); Sana 2012: 'two sets of protein (Hcp2a-VgrG2a (PA1512-PA1511) and Hcp2b-VgrG2b (PA0263-PA0262)) have been proposed as putative H2-T6SS substrates'. Counted as machinery (secreted tube) per the T6SS brief, not as effector.

machinery_genes.role: canonical VgrG spike (puncturing device tip) associated with the PAO1 H2-T6SS tube
machinery_genes.quote: two sets of protein (Hcp2a-VgrG2a (PA1512-PA1511) and Hcp2b-VgrG2b (PA0263-PA0262)) have been proposed as putative H2-T6SS substrates
machinery_genes.note: Sana et al 2012. The PAO1 H2-T6SS core cluster itself carries no vgrG/hcp (noted as 'lacking hcp or vgrG genes within the locus of PAO1'); VgrG2a (PA1511) is the canonical (non-evolved) VgrG spike paired with Hcp2a and is the structural puncturing device. Counted as machinery per the T6SS brief. NOTE: VgrG2b (PA0262), an evolved VgrG, is in this instance's curated EFFECTOR set and is deliberately not listed as machinery here. PA1510 (also in the effector set) sits in the Hcp2a-VgrG2a island.

machinery_genes.role: forkhead-associated (FHA) regulatory protein; phospho-dependent activation of the H2-T6SS apparatus
machinery_genes.quote: As for the H1-T6SS, the H2-T6SS cluster carries stk, stp, and fha genes, which are called stk2, stp2, and fha2 here to acknowledge their link with the H2-T6SS cluster
machinery_genes.note: Jones et al 2014. fha2 is the H2-T6SS Fha/TagH accessory. Conserved between PAO1 and PA14 in the core cluster (Fig 1). Post-translational control component, listed as accessory machinery.

machinery_genes.role: serine-threonine kinase that activates the apparatus by phosphorylating Fha2
machinery_genes.quote: As for the H1-T6SS, the H2-T6SS cluster carries stk, stp, and fha genes, which are called stk2, stp2, and fha2 here to acknowledge their link with the H2-T6SS cluster
machinery_genes.note: Jones et al 2014. stk2 = H2-T6SS serine-threonine kinase (PpkA-type), conserved in the core PAO1/PA14 cluster (Fig 1, purple). Accessory regulatory machinery.

machinery_genes.role: serine-threonine phosphatase that inhibits the apparatus by dephosphorylating Fha2
machinery_genes.quote: As for the H1-T6SS, the H2-T6SS cluster carries stk, stp, and fha genes, which are called stk2, stp2, and fha2 here to acknowledge their link with the H2-T6SS cluster
machinery_genes.note: Jones et al 2014. stp2 = H2-T6SS serine-threonine phosphatase (PppA-type), conserved in the core PAO1/PA14 cluster (Fig 1, purple). Accessory regulatory machinery, antagonist of Stk2.

notes: The H2-T6SS is the second of the three Pseudomonas aeruginosa PAO1 type VI secretion systems (HSI-I/H1, HSI-II/H2, HSI-III/H3), each an inverted contractile phage-tail nanomachine. The H2-T6SS core machinery operon spans PA1656-PA1671 (hsiA2 to clpV2/the distal core genes), is positively regulated by Las/Rhl quorum sensing and repressed by Fur/iron, and promotes PI3K/Akt-dependent internalization of PAO1 into epithelial cells (Sana et al 2012). Gene-naming evidence: Sana 2012 Fig 1 establishes the hsiA2-hsiJ2 / clpV2 / sfa2 naming and hsiA2 = PA1656; Jones 2014 (Filloux lab) provides qRT-PCR-targeted names hsiA2, hsiF2, hsiG2, stk2, stp2, fha2, plus antibody-confirmed HsiB2 and Hcp2 for the conserved core (Fig 1 states the core H2 cluster is conserved between PAO1 and PA14); Lossi 2013 names HsiB2/HsiC2 as the H2 VipA/VipB (TssB/TssC) sheath. Core apparatus covered: sheath (HsiB2/HsiC2 = TssB/TssC), baseplate (HsiF2/HsiG2 = TssF/TssG), ATPase (ClpV2 = TssH), tube (Hcp2 = TssD), spike (VgrG2a = TssI), membrane/first-gene region (HsiA2), and the threonine-phosphorylation accessory triad (Fha2/Stk2/Stp2). SPECIAL CASE per the T6SS brief: Hcp and VgrG ARE machinery here. The PAO1 H2-T6SS CORE locus is unusual in carrying no hcp or vgrG inside the cluster itself (Sana 2012); the cognate Hcp2a-VgrG2a (PA1512-PA1511) and Hcp2b-VgrG2b (PA0263-PA0262) modules sit in adjacent orphan islands. I listed the canonical (non-evolved) VgrG2a as the spike. DELIBERATELY EXCLUDED as effectors (this instance's curated effector set): PA0262 (VgrG2b, an evolved VgrG), PA1510, PA0259/PA0260, PA3290, PA3487 (Tle5/PldA-related phospholipase), PA3905 - these are delivered cargo / evolved-VgrG effectors, not apparatus. Also excluded: RhsP2 and VgrG14 (PA14-specific, not present in PAO1). LOW-CONFIDENCE / NOTES: I did not enumerate every membrane-complex core gene by an individual PA-tag-resolved name (e.g. tssJ2/lip2 = TssJ OM lipoprotein, dotU2 = TssL, icmF2 = TssM, tssE2, tssK2) because no paper gave a clean verbatim naming quote tying those specific names to the H2 instance within the literature searched, and locus-tag resolution is a separate scripted step; the brief's TssJ/TssL/TssM membrane complex is implied present (PA1656-71 contains the full ~13-gene core) but I list only genes with a defensible naming quote per the hard rules. HsiA2's precise unified-family assignment (TssA vs membrane anchor) is approximate; it is firmly the first core gene of the operon. No MacSyFinder/Pfam/HMM scan and no GenBank mining used; all evidence from published Filloux/Mekalanos-lineage literature. Per PubMed attribution requirements, source DOIs are recorded for every gene.
